label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out the correct answer to the question about the Oxford University self-driving car. Let me read through the passage again carefully to make sure I understand the details. The question is asking what makes the car tested by Oxford University different from others. The options are A, B, C, and D. First, let me look at the key differences mentioned in the passage. The first paragraph says the Oxford car is cheaper because it uses off-the-shelf technology and an ordinary iPad. It also mentions that instead of using GPS, it learns routes when a person is driving and then asks if the driver wants it to take over. So right here, there are two points: cheaper (using available tech) and not using GPS. Now, looking at the options: A) use available low-cost technology. That's mentioned in the first paragraph as "off-the-shelf technology" and an iPad. So that seems like a correct point. B) depend on GPS to understand the location. Wait, the passage says the Oxford car does NOT use GPS. Instead, it learns routes when the person is driving. So B is actually the opposite of what the Oxford car does, and more likely what other cars (like Audi, Ford) do. So B is incorrect. C) can choose the places for their owners to visit. The passage says the car learns routes to places the driver frequents, like work, the pub, grocery store. It doesn't say the car chooses the places; it just learns the routes the driver has already taken. So C is incorrect. D) can develop a good relationship with their owners. There's no mention of the car developing a relationship with the owner. The passage talks about the car learning routes and asking to take over, but not about forming a relationship. So D is incorrect. So the correct answer
